rows.sitedescription: The lake occupies a shallow oblong basin, measuring about 150 m by 600 m, between gently sloping hills covered by drift. There are no apparent influents and only a small stream as an outflow. The dominant vegetation on the surrounding slopes is a dwarf-shrub tundra with Betula nana, Empetrum, Salix, Arctostaphylos alpinum, and Rubus chamaemorus as prominent elements. Cottongrass-sedge communities are abundant in wetter depressions.

collectionunits.location: Centre of the main basin of the lake

datasets.datasetnotes: Data contributed by Hyvärinen Hannu.

rows.notes:
We assume that the site is Lake Lommesjön in Björck and Digerfeldt 1991 since number of pollen samples and the depth match the publication (except the last sample that originally was 770, but we assume typing error).
However, another lake also called Lommesjön is found approx. 1 km NE to the one we have marked on the map. This could also be the likely site since position is not clear in the publication.
Another possibility is the unnamed lake positioned betwen the two Lommesjön lakes.

rows.sitedescription: The lake is situated on a bedrock hill in the Alta valley sorne 10 km inland from the fiord head. The surroundinj vegetation is open pine forest grading uphill into mountain birch (Betula pubescens ssp. tortuosa) woodland. The tree line (birch) is at 300 to 400 m. The site is a few kilometres inside the Main Sub-stage (Younger Dryas) end-moraines, and it is weIl above the local marine limit (70-80 m.

rows.sitedescription: Glacial origin lake on Magerøya Island. The local bedrock is principally metamorphosed Late Proterozoic to Cambrian age sandstones, with quartzite, amphibolite and conglomerate layers. Site is located in tundra, a major component of which are dwarf-shrub communities dominated by Empetrum nigrum ssp. hermaphroditum, with Vaccinium vitis-idaea, V. uliginosum, V. myrtillus, and Betula nana as frequent and locally co-dominant components.

collectionunits.collectiondevice: Wright modified square rod piston corer

rows.notes: Called Hestevannet on Google Earth.
rows.sitedescription: Glacial origin lake. Metamorphic rocks, mostly micaceous and garnetiferous schists and slates of Caledonian. age (ca 550–400Ma), predominate the catchment. Local vegetation is tundra, dwarf shrub communities dominated by Empetrum nigrum ssp. hermaphroditum, with Vaccinium spp. and Betula nana.

collectionunits.collectiondevice: Wright modified square rod piston corer

rows.sitedescription: Glacial origin lake lying within the Risvik sub-stage ice limit and just outside the Outer Porsanger moraine that runs along the ridge to the south of the lake. Site is located in tundra with are dwarf-shrub communities dominated by Empetrum nigrum ssp. hermaphroditum, with Vaccinium vitis-idaea, V. uliginosum, V. myrtillus, and Betula nana as frequent and locally co-dominant components. The local bedrock is mostly dark grey sandstone, alternating with layers of grey-black mudstone and shale, assigned to the Barents Sea group and marginal to the rocks of the Caledonian orogeny.

collectionunits.collectiondevice: 7.5 cm diameter Wright-modified square-rod piston corer

rows.sitedescription: Raised bog. Currently being mined for peat.

datasets.datasetnotes: Data were digitized from the pollen diagram in Figure 1, von Post (1918). According to the text, the pollen sum included all trees except Corylus, whose percentages were based on the sum of the other types. The raw digitized tree sums, which varied from 94.5-100.5%, were adjusted to sum to 100%. These percentages were multiplied times a pollen sum of 200 to back calculate the counts. The actual pollen sums were not indicated on the pollen diagram or in the text.

rows.sitedescription:
Lina myr (57° 34′ 31.764″ N, 18° 37′ 59.124″ E), situated 6.3 km southwest of Åminne and 21 km east of Visby, is a 9-km2 area of
cultivated, drained fenland. The area stretches for 6 km from southwest to northeast and 2 km in an east to west direction. Lina myr drains in a northeast direction toward the east coast of
Gotland via the Gothem River. The Gothem River was straightened during the draining of the fen in the 1940s. Today, the elevation
of the Lina myr fen is about 9 m a.s.l. The original basin threshold is estimated to be 9.5–9.9 m a.s.l., based on a cross-section established from modern digital evaluation models (Barliaev, 2017). To the west of the fen the surface geology consists of marl and
further to the west of glacial till (Figure 1c). To the east, limestone bedrock is exposed. The island of Gotland consists of Silurian
limestone (Hede, 1925). The bedrock of Lina myr is classified as the Halla Formation (Laufeld, 1974).

rows.notes: Central part of the province of Småland.
rows.sitedescription: Lake. Physiography: rolling hilly area. Surrounding vegetation: mixed woodlands and small-scale agriculture.

datasets.datasetnotes: Data contributed by Fredh, E.D. to the EPD/Neotoma.

rows.notes: String fen in which the strings are not well developed but rather occur as Sphagnum fuscum hummocks with Betula nana, Empetrum nigrum, Vaccinium uliginosum, V. oxycoccus, Ledum palustre, Andromeda polifolia and Rubus chamaemorus, separated by flark areas dominated by Eriophorum vaginatum, Trichophorum caespitosum and Carex spp. There is one extensive pool, some smaller areas of standing water and a stream that drains the mire towards the east.
rows.sitedescription: Small mire located close to the northern limit of pine forest in eastern Finnish Lapland. To the south, forest areas are dominated almost exclusively by Scots Pine (Pinus sylvestris) while, to the north, the regional vegetation type is mountain birch woodland (Betula pubescens ssp. czerepanovii).

collectionunits.notes: The peat section for the analysis was taken in two parts. A monolith (length: 52 cm) was dug from one of the larger and higher Sphagnum fuscum hummocks in 2005. A second, deeper profile (length: 64 cm) was taken with a Clymo sampler (Clymo, 1988) in 2006 from the same Sphagnum hummock and as close to the monolith location as was possible without introducing contamination.

datasets.datasetnotes: The samples are consecutive and aim at representing around 1 year. Thus, pollen percentages should be usied with caution. We recommend to amalgamate samples together and calculate averages prior to calculating pollen percentages.

rows.sitedescription: Small lake in the hemiboreal vegetation zone dominated by coniferous forest with wide- spread occurrence of deciduous trees, mainly Quercus, Betula, and Populus. It is today a mosaic rural landscape with woodland and agricultural land in varying proportions.

collectionunits.notes: Core sections extracted as 1-m-long core sections in two parallel holes to secure overlap between them.

rows.sitedescription: Lake. Physiography: depression. Surrounding vegetation: P. abies with some P. sylvestris and B. pubescens.

collectionunits.notes: Local vegetation: around the lake herbs (Filipendula ulmaria, Aconitum septentrionale, Valeriana sambucifolia, Saxifraga aizoides and Parnassia palustris). Drier parts are dominated by dwarf shrubs Vaccinium myrtillus, V. vitis-idaea and V. uliginosum, and herbs Potentilla erecta, Melampyrum sylvaticum and Cornus suecica. Carex rostrata, Equisetum fluvatile, Menyanthes trifoliata, Potamogeton natans and Sparganium angustifolium grow in the lake.

datasets.datasetnotes: Data contributed by Bjune, A.E. to the EPD/Neotoma.

rows.sitedescription: Small circular lake. Physiography: glacial origin. Surrounding vegetation: dwarf-shrub vegetation dominated by Calluna vulgaris, Empetrum nigrum, and Betula nana, with some stunted B. pubescens ssp. tortuosa.

datasets.datasetnotes: Dataset contributed by Bjune, A.E. to the EPD/Neotoma.

rows.sitedescription:
Small oval lake. Physiography: small cirque glacier that drained into Lusvatnet during the
Lateglacial. Surrounding vegetation: Open birch woodland dominated by Betula pubescens ssp. tortuosa and with a predominantly ericaceous or tall-fern-dominated understory clothes the steep lower slopes. Above the tree-line, the vegetation is composed of ericaceous dwarf-shrubs and grasses on sheltered slopes, open grass-heath on exposed ridges, and cliff vegetation around the head of the lake where several arctic-alpine and montane species occur.

datasets.datasetnotes: Dataset contributed by Bjune, A.E. to the EPD/Neotoma.
